data.title: Anheuser-Busch InBev reports mixed Q1 results; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Anheuser-Busch InBev press release (BUD): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.81 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $13.63B (-6.3% Y/Y) misses by $180M.
  • Volumes declined by 2.2%, with beer volumes down by 2.5% and non-beer volumes down by 0.2%.
  • 53% increase in Gross Merchandise Value from sales of third-party products through BEES Marketplace to reach 645 million USD.
  • In 1Q25, Normalized EBITDA increased by 7.9% to 4 855 million USD with a margin expansion of 218bps to 35.6%.

  • 2025 Outlook

    (i) Overall Performance: We expect our EBITDA to grow in line with our medium-term outlook of between 4-8%. The outlook for FY25 reflects our current assessment of inflation and other macroeconomic conditions.

    (ii) Net Finance Costs: Net pension interest expenses and accretion expenses are expected to be in the range of 190 to 220 million USD per quarter, depending on currency and interest rate fluctuations. We expect the average gross debt coupon in FY25 to be approximately 4%.

    (iii) Effective Tax Rate: We expect the normalized ETR in FY25 to be in the range of 26% to 28%. The ETR outlook does not consider the impact of potential future changes in legislation.

    (iv) Net Capital Expenditure: We expect net capital expenditure of between 3.5 and 4.0 billion USD in FY25.

data.title: Genco Shipping slashes dividend by 50% to $0.15
data.description:
• Genco Shipping (NYSE:GNK) declares $0.15/share quarterly dividend, -50% decrease from prior dividend of $0.30.
  • Forward yield 4.46%

  • See GNK Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Argenx reports mixed Q1 results; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Argenx SE press release (NASDAQ:ARGX): Q1 GAAP EPS of $2.58 beats by $0.13.
  • Revenue of $814.56M (+103.1% Y/Y) misses by $23.85M.

  • FINANCIAL GUIDANCE

    The financial guidance on the combined selling, general and administrative expenses and research and development expenses remains unchanged at approximately $2.5 billion.

data.title: Equinox Gold Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.08 misses by $0.19, revenue of $423.7M beats by $19.7M
data.description:
• Equinox Gold press release (NYSE:EQX): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.08 misses by $0.19.
  • Revenue of $423.7M (+75.6% Y/Y) beats by $19.7M.
  • Produced 145,290 ounces of gold.
  • Sold 147,920 ounces of gold at an average realized gold price of $2,858 per oz.
  • Total cash costs of $1,769 per oz and AISC of $2,065 per oz.
  • Cash flow from operations before changes in non-cash working capital of $73.3 million ($54.5 million after changes in non-cash working capital).
  • Mine-site free cash flow before changes in non-cash working capital of $57.7 million ($38.8 million after changes in non-cash working capital).
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $137.9 million.
  • Guidance: Provided 2025 production and cost guidance of 635,000 to 750,000 ounces of gold at cash costs of $1,075 to $1,175 per oz and AISC of $1,455 to $1,550 per oz(2). Guidance does not include any production from Los Filos or Castle Mountain.
  • Provided 2025 sustaining and non-sustaining expenditure guidance of $412 million, comprising $310 million of sustaining expenditures and $102 million of non-sustaining expenditures.

data.title: First Majestic Silver Non-GAAP EPS of $0.05 misses by $0.04, revenue of $243.9M
data.description:
• First Majestic Silver press release (NYSE:AG): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.05 misses by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $243.9M (+130.1% Y/Y).
  • The company ended the quarter with the highest cash and restricted cash balance in its history of $462.6 million consisting of $351.3 million in cash and cash equivalents and $111.3 million in restricted cash. 
  • The company produced 7.7 million silver equivalent ounces in Q1 2025 representing 26% of the mid-point of the Company's 2025 production guidance, including a quarterly silver production record of 3.7 million silver ounces, an 88% increase when compared t.o 2.0 million silver ounces produced in Q1 2024. Gold production also increased by 1% to 36,469 ounces.
  • Operating cash flow before changes in working capital and taxes in the quarter was a record $110.0 million, representing a significant increase compared to $12.6 million in the first quarter of 2024.
  • Free cash flow for the quarter totaled $43.5 million.
  • Consolidated AISC in the first quarter was $19.24 per AgEq ounce, representing an 11% improvement from $21.53 per AgEq ounce in the first quarter of 2024, and 7% below the mid-point of 2025 cost guidance.

data.title: Sandisk Corporation Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.30 beats by $0.09, revenue of $1.7B beats by $73.1M
data.description:
• Sandisk Corporation press release (NASDAQ:SNDK): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.30 beats by $0.09.
  • Revenue of $1.7B beats by $73.1M.
  • Expect fiscal fourth quarter 2025 revenue to be in the range of $1.75 billion to $1.85 billion.
  • Expect Non-GAAP earnings (loss) per share to be in the range of ($0.10) to $0.15.

data.title: Fortuna Silver Mines Non-GAAP EPS of $0.20 misses by $0.01, revenue of $290.1M misses by $0.9M
data.description:
• Fortuna Silver Mines press release (NYSE:FSM): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.20 misses by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $290.1M (+44.4% Y/Y) misses by $0.9M.
  • Record free cash flow from ongoing operations of $111.3 million in Q1, a quarter over quarter increase of 30%. 
  • Gold equivalent production of 103,459 ounces3 in Q1.
  • Consolidated cash cost per GEO1 from continuing operations of $929 in Q1, down from $1,015 in Q4 2024 (excluding San Jose from the comparative period cash cost is up from $888 in Q4 2024).
  • Consolidated AISC per GEO1 from continuing operations of $1,640 for Q1 down from $1,772 in Q4 2024 (excluding San Jose from the comparative period AISC is down from $1,690 in Q4 2024).

data.title: Energy Fuels reports mixed Q1 results; updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Energy Fuels press release (NYSE:UUUU): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.13 misses by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $16.9M (-33.5% Y/Y) beats by $8.72M.
  • As of March 31, 2025, the Company had $214.61 million of working capital including $73.00 million of cash and cash equivalents, $89.64 million of marketable securities (interest-bearing securities and uranium stocks).
  • During the three months ended March 31, 2025, the Company sold 6,836 tonnes of rutile, 12,852 tonnes of ilmenite, both used for the production of titanium products, and 1,429 tonnes of zircon, used for the production of zirconium, for total HMS revenues of $15.54 million.
  • The company produced a total of 150,000 pounds of finished U3O8 during the three months ended March 31, 2025 from stockpiled alternate feed materials and newly mined ore.
  • The Company is pleased to provide its updated production, sales and inventory guidance for 2025 as follows:
